questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: When you get angry, you usually

choices.c: leave the situation and engage yourself in a different activity

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Your maid has invited you to her daughter's wedding. You would [Hotel Mgmt 2008]

choices.c: buy a gift for her daughter and help in wedding
choices.d: congratulate her and make up some excuse for not being able to attend

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: When you see a blind man trying to cross the road, you

choices.a: wait till he crosses the road

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are walking down the street and suddenly you see two hundred rupee notes on the pavement. What action will you take?

choices.a: Deposit it in the nearest police station
choices.d: Give the money to a beggar

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Your bathroom tap is leaking and is a constant source of irritating noise. You would

choices.a: sleep with pillows upon your ears
choices.c: call a plumber to repair the tap
choices.d: try to put up a cork upon the mouth of the tap

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are having tea in your office, some of the tea spills on your clothes. You will [CG PSC 2013]

choices.a: call the peon to wipe off the tea from your clothes
choices.b: yourself wipe off the tea from your clothes
choices.d: ask the peon to fetch clothes from your house

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If in an examination hall, you find the question paper is too tough to be answered satisfactorily by you, the best thing to do for you is to [NIFT (UG) 2005]

choices.a: tell the examiner that the questions are out of course
choices.b: provoke the candidates to walk out of the examination hall
choices.c: try to know something from your neighbour
choices.d: try to solve the questions as much as you know with a cool head

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are a member of the sports team of your college. One day due to some misunderstanding, other members stop talking to you. You

choices.b: wait till they come and start talking again
choices.c: keep to yourself and let things take their time for improving

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are a guest at a dinner. The host asks you to take one more chapatti after your stomach is full. You would

choices.a: make a bad face at him
choices.c: politely say that the food was too good and you have already eaten much

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are head of your office. Some media people come to your office and request you to brief them about the pension plan of your office. You will [CG PSC 2013]

choices.a: ask the media people to come some another day
choices.b: immediately accept the offer and brief them
choices.c: get them turned out of your office
choices.d: ask them to wait and to consult the officials concerned

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Your classmate, who got you in a fix recently with a teacher, has met with an accident. You

choices.a: feel that God taught him/her a lesson
choices.c: tell others that this is the way one suffers for making others suffer
choices.d: decide to visit him/her in the hospital
